refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall enable the operator (on-site or remote) to execute earthmoving tasks with measurable productivity of ≥ productivity_target (m³/h or ton/h) during representative operations over the agreed evaluation period, while maintaining compliance with worksite safety plan and operational policies.
refined_fr.rationale: CR-NGHE-001 is a high-level acceptance criterion that conflates productivity measurement with operational capability. Refinement separates the functional requirement (enable task execution at target productivity) from the verification criterion (measured KPI ≥ target). This clarifies that productivity is an emergent property dependent on vehicle capability, operator effectiveness (on-site or remote), and task execution fidelity. High safety criticality requires explicit safety compliance linkage.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Measure actual productivity (m³/h or ton/h) during representative operations under controlled conditions; cross-reference against worksite safety plan compliance logs and operational policy adherence records.
sub_frs: FR-NGHE-001.1: The vehicle shall execute earthmoving tasks (dig, load, transport, dump) with sufficient speed and precision to achieve target productivity.
FR-NGHE-001.2: The operator interface (on-site or teleoperation) shall provide sufficient situational awareness and control responsiveness to enable target productivity.
FR-NGHE-001.3: The NGHE solution shall enforce safety constraints (speed limits, geofencing, load limits) that do not degrade productivity below target when operated within worksite safety plan.
FR-NGHE-001.4: The NGHE solution shall provide real-time productivity telemetry and task status to the Site Manager for performance monitoring and optimization.

candidate_dps: DP-A: Integrate high-performance hydraulic actuation with optimized cycle-time control logic and operator feedback to maximize task throughput while enforcing safety constraints via real-time monitoring.
DP-B: Deploy a modular task execution framework (dig, load, transport, dump) with adaptive cycle timing and safety-aware scheduling to achieve target productivity across variable worksite conditions.

DP-C: Implement a closed-loop productivity optimization system combining vehicle performance telemetry, operator input, safety constraint enforcement, and adaptive task sequencing to maintain ≥ target productivity.
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP-C: Implement a closed-loop productivity optimization system combining vehicle performance telemetry, operator input, safety constraint enforcement, and adaptive task sequencing to maintain ≥ target productivity.

potential_sources: Vehicle actuation performance (hydraulic response time) couples to operator control responsiveness.
Safety constraint enforcement (speed, load limits) couples to task cycle timing.
Telemetry collection couples to real-time monitoring and Site Manager visibility.
Teleoperation latency couples to operator effectiveness and achievable productivity.
coupling_check.notes: DP-C acknowledges these couplings explicitly by integrating telemetry feedback, safety enforcement, and adaptive sequencing. Coupling is managed through closed-loop control rather than eliminated. This is appropriate for a high-safety-criticality system where safety constraints must actively participate in productivity optimization.

assumptions_risks: Assumption: Vehicle actuation system can respond to control commands within operator reaction time (on-site) or network latency + control loop time (teleoperation).
Assumption: Worksite safety plan constraints (speed limits, geofencing, load limits) are quantifiable and can be encoded as real-time enforcement rules.
Risk: If teleoperation network latency exceeds ~500 ms, operator effectiveness may degrade below target productivity; mitigation requires autonomous task sequencing fallback.
Risk: If safety constraint enforcement is too conservative, productivity may fall below target; requires iterative tuning during commissioning.
Risk: Adaptive task sequencing logic may conflict with operator intent if not properly coordinated; requires clear authority model (operator override vs. system recommendation).
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Clarify the authority model: Does the operator have override capability for safety-constrained task sequencing, or is the system fully autonomous within safety bounds? This affects both DP feasibility and IM assignment (whether Teleoperation or Vehicle owns the sequencing logic).

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: The NGHE Solution is the top-level integration boundary that orchestrates vehicle performance, teleoperation capability, safety services, and productivity monitoring. It owns the closed-loop productivity optimization logic, real-time constraint enforcement, and telemetry aggregation that collectively enable target productivity while maintaining safety compliance.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: NGHE Solution emits productivity metrics (m³/h, ton/h, cycle time), vehicle state (position, load, fuel), and safety status to Site Manager and monitoring systems.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: NGHE Solution receives task directives (dig location, load target, dump location) from operator (on-site or remote via Teleoperation) and Site Manager.

external_interfaces.name: SafetyAlert
external_interfaces.details: NGHE Solution emits safety alerts (constraint violations, geofence breaches, load limit exceeded) to Safety Manager and operator.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: NGHE Solution communicates with Teleoperation module for remote operator commands and with Safety Services for real-time constraint validation.

module_containment_scores.notes: NGHE Solution is the system-level integration boundary. The closed-loop productivity optimization logic, telemetry aggregation, and constraint enforcement orchestration naturally reside here. Minimal diffusion: only Vehicle-specific actuation control and Teleoperation-specific latency compensation remain distributed.

Vehicle owns actuation performance and cycle-time execution, but does not own the closed-loop optimization logic or safety constraint enforcement policy. If productivity optimization were pushed entirely to Vehicle, it would duplicate logic across multiple vehicle types and lose Site Manager visibility. High diffusion risk.

Teleoperation owns operator interface and command transmission, but not the productivity optimization logic itself. If Teleoperation owned optimization, it would be tightly coupled to network latency and operator skill, making it difficult to achieve consistent target productivity. Significant diffusion risk.

Safety Services owns constraint definition and validation, but not the productivity optimization logic that must respect those constraints. If Safety Services owned optimization, it would blur the boundary between safety enforcement and productivity optimization, complicating both. High diffusion risk.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: The NGHE Solution module is the appropriate owner of the closed-loop productivity optimization system because: (1) it has system-level visibility into vehicle state, operator input, safety constraints, and Site Manager requirements; (2) it can aggregate telemetry from Vehicle and Teleoperation to drive adaptive task sequencing; (3) it can enforce safety constraints in real-time without duplicating logic across Vehicle or Teleoperation; (4) it provides a single point of control for productivity KPI measurement and reporting.
containment_risks: Risk: If NGHE Solution optimization logic becomes too complex or tightly coupled to specific vehicle types, it may be difficult to extend to new vehicle models or task types. Mitigation: Use a modular, policy-based optimization framework (e.g., constraint satisfaction or reinforcement learning) that can be retrained for new contexts.

Risk: If NGHE Solution does not have sufficient real-time performance (latency, throughput), it may not be able to enforce safety constraints or adapt task sequencing fast enough. Mitigation: Allocate strict latency budgets (e.g., <100 ms for constraint enforcement) and validate via simulation and field testing.

Risk: If NGHE Solution optimization logic conflicts with operator intent (e.g., operator wants to dig in a location that the system deems unsafe), it may create confusion or unsafe workarounds. Mitigation: Implement clear authority model (operator override with safety confirmation, or system recommendation with operator acceptance).
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Explicitly define the optimization policy: Is the NGHE Solution responsible for autonomous task sequencing (e.g., 'dig here, then load, then dump'), or does it only optimize cycle timing within operator-directed tasks? This affects the scope of the IM boundary and the interfaces with Teleoperation and Vehicle.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The refined FR (enable task execution at target productivity while maintaining safety compliance) is directly addressed by DP-C (closed-loop productivity optimization with safety constraint enforcement). The DP is specific enough to guide IM assignment and detailed design, yet abstract enough to accommodate multiple implementation strategies (e.g., rule-based, learning-based, or hybrid). The coupling check confirms that DP-C acknowledges and manages the key couplings (actuation, safety, telemetry, teleoperation latency) rather than ignoring them.

diffusion_risks: Diffusion Risk 1: If the closed-loop optimization logic is split between NGHE Solution and Vehicle (e.g., Vehicle owns cycle-time optimization, NGHE Solution owns task sequencing), the two modules may have conflicting optimization objectives. Mitigation: Establish a clear interface contract (e.g., Vehicle exposes cycle-time capability, NGHE Solution uses it to plan task sequences).

Diffusion Risk 2: If telemetry collection is distributed across Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services, NGHE Solution may not have a unified view of system state. Mitigation: Require all modules to emit telemetry to a common Telemetry interface owned by NGHE Solution.

Diffusion Risk 3: If safety constraint enforcement is split between Safety Services (policy definition) and NGHE Solution (real-time enforcement), there may be inconsistencies or delays. Mitigation: Safety Services defines constraints; NGHE Solution enforces them in real-time with periodic validation against Safety Services.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: NGHE Solution is the appropriate owner of the closed-loop optimization logic because it has system-level visibility and can avoid duplication. Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services remain focused on their core responsibilities (actuation, operator interface, constraint definition) and provide interfaces to NGHE Solution. This minimizes diffusion and maintains clear ownership boundaries.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification of the refined FR is feasible: (1) Productivity KPI can be measured directly during representative operations (m³/h or ton/h). (2) Safety compliance can be verified against worksite safety plan logs and operational policy records. (3) Telemetry from NGHE Solution provides evidence of constraint enforcement and task execution. (4) Field testing can validate that target productivity is achieved under representative conditions.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: The allocation of FR to NGHE Solution is clear: NGHE Solution is responsible for orchestrating the closed-loop optimization system that enables target productivity. Sub-FRs are allocated to Vehicle (task execution), Teleoperation (operator interface), Safety Services (constraint enforcement), and NGHE Solution (optimization and monitoring). Each allocation is traceable to a specific module and interface.

recommended_revisions: Revision 1: Clarify the authority model for task sequencing (operator override vs. system autonomy) before detailed design. This affects both DP and IM scope.
Revision 2: Define quantitative latency budgets for real-time constraint enforcement (e.g., <100 ms) and validate feasibility during DP-to-IM transition.
Revision 3: Establish a telemetry schema and interface contract between NGHE Solution and Vehicle/Teleoperation/Safety Services to ensure unified system state visibility.
Revision 4: Specify the optimization policy (rule-based, learning-based, or hybrid) and validate that it can achieve target productivity across representative worksite conditions and vehicle types.

decision.justification: The refined FR is well-defined, the selected DP is sound and addresses the key couplings, and the IM assignment to NGHE Solution is appropriate with clear interfaces. However, the DP contains unresolved assumptions about the authority model (operator override vs. system autonomy) and the optimization policy (rule-based vs. learning-based). These must be resolved before proceeding to detailed design. Additionally, the sub-FRs (FR-NGHE-001.1 through FR-NGHE-001.4) require their own FR-DP-IM decomposition to ensure that Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services are properly designed to support the NGHE Solution's closed-loop optimization. Proceeding to the next level will clarify these dependencies and validate the architecture.
next_level_sub_frs: FR-NGHE-001.1: The vehicle shall execute earthmoving tasks (dig, load, transport, dump) with sufficient speed and precision to achieve target productivity.
FR-NGHE-001.2: The operator interface (on-site or teleoperation) shall provide sufficient situational awareness and control responsiveness to enable target productivity.
FR-NGHE-001.3: The NGHE solution shall enforce safety constraints (speed limits, geofencing, load limits) that do not degrade productivity below target when operated within worksite safety plan.
FR-NGHE-001.4: The NGHE solution shall provide real-time productivity telemetry and task status to the Site Manager for performance monitoring and optimization.

revise.why: Before decomposing sub-FRs, resolve the authority model and optimization policy in DP-C. Specifically: (1) Define whether the operator has override capability for safety-constrained task sequencing, or whether the system is fully autonomous within safety bounds. (2) Specify the optimization policy (rule-based constraint satisfaction, learning-based reinforcement learning, or hybrid). (3) Quantify latency budgets for real-time constraint enforcement and teleoperation. These clarifications will guide the DP-to-IM mapping for sub-FRs and ensure that Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services are designed with the correct responsibilities.

assumptions: Assumption 1: Vehicle actuation system can respond to control commands within operator reaction time (on-site, ~200 ms) or network latency + control loop time (teleoperation, ~500 ms).
Assumption 2: Worksite safety plan constraints (speed limits, geofencing, load limits) are quantifiable and can be encoded as real-time enforcement rules.
Assumption 3: Operator (on-site or remote) has sufficient skill and situational awareness to execute tasks at target productivity when provided with adequate feedback and control responsiveness.

Assumption 4: Site Manager has access to real-time telemetry and can make optimization decisions (e.g., task prioritization, resource allocation) that improve productivity without compromising safety.

Assumption 5: The NGHE Solution can achieve target productivity across representative worksite conditions (soil type, terrain, weather) and vehicle types (excavator, loader, dozer) without requiring extensive retuning.
open_risks: Risk 1: If teleoperation network latency exceeds ~500 ms, operator effectiveness may degrade below target productivity. Mitigation: Implement autonomous task sequencing fallback or accept lower productivity targets for remote operations.
Risk 2: If safety constraint enforcement is too conservative, productivity may fall below target. Mitigation: Iterative tuning during commissioning with Site Manager and Safety Manager oversight.

Risk 3: If the authority model (operator override vs. system autonomy) is not clearly defined, operators may create unsafe workarounds or ignore system recommendations. Mitigation: Implement clear UI/UX for authority model and conduct operator training.

Risk 4: If the optimization policy is too complex or opaque, Site Manager and operators may not trust the system's recommendations. Mitigation: Use interpretable optimization methods (e.g., rule-based) and provide clear explanations for system decisions.

Risk 5: If NGHE Solution optimization logic is tightly coupled to specific vehicle types or task types, it may be difficult to extend to new contexts. Mitigation: Use modular, policy-based optimization framework.
evidence_needed: Evidence 1: Field testing data showing actual productivity (m³/h or ton/h) achieved during representative operations with the NGHE Solution.
Evidence 2: Safety compliance logs showing that worksite safety plan constraints were enforced and no violations occurred during operations.
Evidence 3: Telemetry data showing real-time constraint enforcement, task sequencing, and productivity optimization decisions.
Evidence 4: Operator feedback on situational awareness, control responsiveness, and trust in the system's recommendations.
Evidence 5: Latency measurements for teleoperation network, control loop, and constraint enforcement to validate that they meet the assumed budgets.
Evidence 6: Validation that the optimization policy achieves target productivity across representative worksite conditions and vehicle types.
